title: The Lost-and-Found Desk
summary: At a busy train station, a small desk full of lost things tells quiet stories about people.
body:
Mei works at the lost-and-found desk in a big train station in Singapore. People bring her things they find. People also come to look for things they lost. The desk is small, but it holds many stories.

On the shelf there are many umbrellas. People lose umbrellas more than anything else. Mei thinks this is funny. People hold an umbrella to stay dry. But when the rain stops, they forget it at once. The umbrella did its job, so the mind lets it go.

Some things are different. One day a man comes back for an old, cheap watch. It does not look special. But the man is almost crying. The watch was his father's. Mei learns something. A thing is not lost because it costs a lot. It is lost because someone loves it.

Most people never come back. The shelf fills up with keys, gloves, and toys. Mei keeps them for a long time. She hopes someone will return. The desk is quiet, but it is full of small hopes. Every lost thing is a person who was here, and then went away.

annotations.meaningVi: đã làm xong việc của nó

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives with 'more than'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'more than' để so sánh số lượng nhiều hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We compare amounts with more than: "People lose umbrellas more than anything else."

grammarNotes.title: because for reasons
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để giải thích lý do.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Because gives a reason, as in "It is lost because someone loves it."

grammarNotes.title: Past simple of irregular verbs
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Một số động từ quá khứ không thêm -ed (come → came, go → went).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Some past forms are irregular, such as "a person who was here, and then went away."

translationVi:
Mei làm việc ở quầy đồ thất lạc trong một nhà ga lớn ở Singapore. Người ta mang đến cho cô những thứ họ nhặt được. Người ta cũng đến tìm những thứ họ đánh mất. Quầy thì nhỏ, nhưng nó chứa nhiều câu chuyện.

Trên kệ có rất nhiều cái ô. Người ta đánh mất ô nhiều hơn bất cứ thứ gì khác. Mei thấy điều này buồn cười. Người ta cầm ô để không bị ướt. Nhưng khi mưa tạnh, họ quên nó ngay lập tức. Cái ô đã làm xong việc của nó, nên đầu óc buông nó ra.

Một số thứ thì khác. Một ngày nọ, một người đàn ông quay lại tìm một chiếc đồng hồ cũ, rẻ tiền. Nó trông không có gì đặc biệt. Nhưng người đàn ông gần như khóc. Chiếc đồng hồ là của cha ông. Mei học được một điều. Một thứ không phải là quý vì nó đắt tiền. Nó quý vì có người yêu thương nó.

Hầu hết mọi người không bao giờ quay lại. Kệ đầy dần lên với chìa khóa, găng tay và đồ chơi. Mei giữ chúng trong một thời gian dài. Cô hy vọng ai đó sẽ trở lại. Quầy thì yên tĩnh, nhưng nó đầy những hy vọng nhỏ bé. Mỗi thứ thất lạc là một con người đã từng ở đây, rồi sau đó rời đi.

options: At a lost-and-found desk in a train station
questions.explanation: "Mei works at the lost-and-found desk in a big train station in Singapore."

questions.prompt: What do people do at the desk?
options: Bring things they find and look for things they lost
questions.explanation: "People bring her things they find. People also come to look for things they lost."

questions.prompt: Which thing do people lose the most?
questions.explanation: "People lose umbrellas more than anything else."

questions.prompt: Why do people forget umbrellas, the passage suggests?
options: When the rain stops, the umbrella did its job
questions.explanation: "when the rain stops, they forget it at once. The umbrella did its job, so the mind lets it go."

questions.prompt: What does the man come back for?
questions.explanation: "a man comes back for an old, cheap watch."

questions.prompt: Why is the watch important to the man?
What does Mei learn from the watch?
options: A thing is lost because someone loves it, not because it is expensive
questions.explanation: "A thing is not lost because it costs a lot. It is lost because someone loves it."

questions.prompt: What happens to most lost things?
What does "the desk is full of small hopes" mean?
options: Mei hopes each lost thing finds its owner
questions.explanation: "She hopes someone will return. The desk is quiet, but it is full of small hopes."
questions.hint: Last paragraph; think about the meaning.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Lost things show us what people truly care about
questions.explanation: The contrast of the watch and the umbrellas shows that value comes from love, not price.

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the last sentence?
questions.explanation: "Every lost thing is a person who was here, and then went away" feels quiet and reflective.

questions.prompt: The phrase "costs a lot" means something is...
questions.explanation: "A thing is not lost because it costs a lot" — it is not about being expensive.

blanks.explanation: "a man comes back for an old, cheap watch."
"Mei keeps them for a long time."

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mei says a thing is lost "because someone loves it," not because it costs a lot. Do you agree that love, not price, makes an object important? Why?
